title: luma sticheromischa
side_effects: Luma sticheromischa can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory issues. Ingesting the plant's leaves or flowers can cause nausea, vomiting, and stomach pain due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can lead to inflammation and blistering of the skin, potentially resulting in scarring. Furthermore, improper use of the plant can also cause headaches, dizziness, and fatigue in some individuals.
